info.title: Inter.link Portal API
info.description:
### Welcome to the Inter.link API. 

Disrupting the network industry by providing better, sustainable, and automated connectivity to the Internet.

Please refer to [Getting Started](https://inter.link/knowledgebase/api/) for more information.

get.description: Get all API keys for the current user.

post.description:
Create a new API key for the current user.

You should store the key somewhere safe: you will not be able to see the key again

get.description: Get an API key for the current user.

delete.description: Delete an API key for the current user.

get.description: Get the current two-factor authentication enforcement status for the user's organization.

post.description:
One-time deferral: hide the two-factor authentication enforcement dialog until the next login.
After that, the user must enable two-factor authentication.

put.description:
Toggle two-factor authentication enforcement for the user's organization.
This requires appropriate permissions to modify company settings. (coming soon...)

post.description: Purchase a new Flex Ethernet Service
Purchase a new FlexPeer Service endpoint (local/requester).

Purchase a new FlexPeer Service endpoint (remote/peer).
This endpoint requires a flexconnect_key from a local/requester endpoint.
Purchase a new FlexPeer Service that connects to a premium peer.
Purchase a new IP Access Service

get.description:
Search for locations

Args:
    name: Location's name, multiple comma-separated values are allowed
    text: free-text search over name, address and description
    country: Location's country code, multiple comma-separated values are allowed
    within_circle: Geographic circle where the locations are contained. The format is: center_latitude,center_longitude,radius
    within_rectangle: Geographic rectangle where the locations are contained.
        The format is: northeast_latitude,northeast_longitude,southwest_latitude,southwest_longitude

List services

If `service_id` is provided, only services that are part of that service are returned.

If `product_id` is provided, only services that are part of that product are returned.
You can provide a list of product ids to filter by multiple products. E.g. `product_id=PID19,PID44`.

If `service_component_id` is provided, only services that are part of that service component are returned.

If `include_decommissioned` is true, decommissioned services are included in the list (default is false).

put.summary: Create Or Refresh Flex Connect Key

get.description:
List service components

Arguments:

* `service_id`: if provided, only components for that service are returned.
* `product_component_id`: if provided, only components for that product component are returned. Multiple product component ids can be provided as a comma-separated list.
* `show_in_progress`: If False, only components that are completely provisioned are returned.
* `exclude_dependency`: If true, the componenets that have a dependency are not returned

See also [/services](#/service/common_services_api_list_services) for a list of services.

List LOA documents

If `service_id` is provided, return the LOA document for that service.
If `service_component_id` is provided, return the LOA document for that service component.
Else, return all LOA documents.

put.description:
Updates the current Company's profile infor of the current user. Fails if the user
is not associated to that Company.

patch.description:
Updates the current Company's profile infor of the current user. Fails if the user
is not associated to that Company.

get.description:
Returns a list of companies that the current user has access to.
Empty list if the user has no access to any company.

Returns
-------
List[CustomerModel]
    List of companies that the current user has access to.
    [0] Current company data.
    [*_] Tenants companies data.

post.description:
Create a new Customer for the current user. Fails if the user
is already associated to a customer.
Creates tenant for user Company. Fails if the user is not associated to a customer.

get.description: Get all invoices for the current company.

Returns all users that are associated with the current customer.

* A customer is an organization. There are no user-accounts for organizations.
* A user is a real person with an account.

post.description: Creates a new user account and associates this new user with the current customer.

put.description:
Update an existing user.

Note, if you're not associated with a customer, you can only update your own user.

get.description:
This endpoint explicitly checks if a password reset token is valid without
requiring the user to actually reset the password.

This immediate validation improves UX by allowing the frontend to instantly
inform users if their password reset link is expired or invalid,
instead of forcing them to submit a new password before being notified
of token invalidity (which is the default behavior of django-allauth).

Lists Firewalls

If `elan_id` is provided, only firewalls that are part of that ELAN are returned.
Returns the Inter.link status feed (trimmed) and maintenance summary flags.
